perturbation.original_text: Receipt of Information. Ameren Illinois disclose to Recipient, customer information protected from public disclosure pursuant to 815 ILCS 505/2HH (“Confidential Information”) for use by the Recipient and its officers, directors, agents, employees and contractors (collectively, the “Representatives”), on a need-to-know basis for the purpose of facilitating electric supply aggregation pursuant to 20 ILCS 3855/1-92.
perturbation.changed_text: Receipt of Information. Ameren Illinois disclose to Recipient, customer data shielded from public access under 815 ILCS 505/2HH (“Confidential Information”) for use by the Recipient and its officers, directors, agents, employees and contractors (collectively, the “Representatives”), on a need-to-know basis for the purpose of facilitating electric supply aggregation pursuant to 20 ILCS 3855/1-92.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to “customer information protected from public disclosure”. The modified text changes this to “customer data shielded from public access”. While seemingly similar, 'information' and 'data' can have different legal interpretations, especially concerning privacy laws. This creates uncertainty as to the scope of what is considered 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information Defined. The Parties acknowledge that any such information referred to in paragraph above shall be considered Confidential Information. Under this Agreement, all information disclosed by the Ameren Illinois whether provided in oral, written, visual, electronic or other form is presumed to be Confidential Information unless it falls within one of the exclusions of Section 3. Confidential Information includes non-public customer specific information. Confidential Information as used herein also includes information supplied by Ameren Illinois to Recipient prior to the execution of this Agreement, and such Confidential Information shall be considered in the same manner and be subject to the same treatment as the Confidential Information made available after the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'customer information protected from public disclosure' to 'customer data shielded from public access'. The perturbation explanation notes that 'information' and 'data' have different legal interpretations, creating uncertainty about the scope of 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text states that 'all information disclosed by Ameren Illinois... is presumed to be Confidential Information unless it falls within one of the exclusions of Section 3' and includes 'non-public customer specific information'. The change introduces ambiguity regarding whether data is included as information, which leads to an in-text contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information includes non-public customer specific information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information includes private consumer specific details.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses “non-public customer specific information”. The modified text replaces this with “private consumer specific details.” Switching 'customer' to 'consumer' and 'information' to 'details' introduces ambiguity. 'Customer' generally implies a business relationship, while 'consumer' may have broader implications. Similarly, 'details' is less specific than 'information,' leading to confusion regarding the precise scope of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure Obligation. The Recipient shall maintain the confidentiality of any Confidential I nformation and shall not disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any person other than its Representatives who need to know such Confidential Information. Representatives shall be informed by the Recipient of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall be directed by the Recipient to treat the Confidential Information confidentially, except with the prior written consent of Ameren Illinois or as otherwise permitted hereunder. Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representative.
perturbation.justification: The change from "non-public customer specific information" to "private consumer specific details" introduces ambiguity in the definition of Confidential Information. The contradicted text refers to "Confidential Information" and imposes non-disclosure obligations. Since 'customer' is more specific than 'consumer', and 'information' more precise than 'details', the modified text broadens the scope of confidential information potentially beyond what was originally intended and understood within the context of the Non-Disclosure Obligation. This introduces ambiguity regarding what information is subject to the non-disclosure agreement, thus creating a direct conflict, especially because the contract does not define these terms. It creates a Misaligned Terminology In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either of the Parties may terminate the exchange of Confidential Information u nder this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other Party specifically referencing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Either of the Associated Entities may end the sharing of Private Details u nder this Agreement at any time by written notification to the other Participant clearly mentioning this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses “Parties”, "terminate the exchange of Confidential Information" and "written notice". The modified text now uses "Associated Entities", "end the sharing of Private Details" and "written notification" instead. The redefinition of parties at the top coupled with further changes creates ambiguity if this termination applies to them. The term “Private Details” is not formally defined, creating confusion. Furthermore, 'notice' is replaced by 'notification', 'referencing' is changed to 'mentioning' which create uncertainty in procedure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Waiver; Amendment; Exclusive Agreement. No failure or delay by either of the Parties in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege hereunder. This Agreement shall not be modified, supplemented or amended except by a writing signed by both Parties hereto. This Agreement represents the exclusive agreement between the Parties in connection with the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Associated Entities' and 'terminate the exchange of Confidential Information' with 'end the sharing of Private Details'. This introduces ambiguity in the 'Contradicted Text' which refers to 'Parties' and 'This Agreement'. Specifically, it becomes unclear whether the termination clause applies to the redefined 'Associated Entities', creating a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding the agreement's modification and exclusivity, as the 'Contradicted Text' states that the agreement can only be modified by a writing signed by both 'Parties'. Since the term 'Private Details' is undefined, it also contradicts the original agreement terms. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of misaligned terminology.
